D1 Optics L6 Create Reference

Second-Harmonic Generation

drive a nonlinear crystal hard enough to double the frequency and turn infrared to green

A strong field in a χ² crystal radiates at twice its frequency. The doubled waves add up only if they stay in step with the fundamental — phase matching. Otherwise the output oscillates as a sinc².

Set the input intensity, crystal length, and phase mismatch Δk·L. The crystal turns infrared green when intensity is high and phase-matched; the curve shows the sinc² acceptance.
